content: The Global Reporting Initiative (GRI) Standards provide a framework for organisations to report on their economic, environmental, and social impacts. GRI requires a materiality assessment to identify which topics are most significant. For Canadian SMBs, key material topics typically include: GRI 302 (Energy), GRI 305 (Emissions), GRI 306 (Waste), GRI 401 (Employment), GRI 403 (Occupational Health and Safety), GRI 405 (Diversity and Equal Opportunity), and GRI 418 (Customer Privacy). Companies are expected to report using the 'in accordance' option, disclosing universal standards, sector standards where applicable, and topic-specific disclosures for each material topic. GRI-aligned reporting strengthens credibility with investors, procurement partners, and regulators.
